title: hieracium serratifrons subsp. stenstroemii
intro: Hieracium serratifrons subsp. stenstroemii, also known as Hawkweed or Hieracium stenstroemii, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, native to mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to aid in wound healing and reducing pain. The plant's leaves and roots are used for various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, Hieracium serratifrons subsp. stenstroemii is often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
